name: Salvation Army Veteran & Family Assistance
description: Emergency financial assistance for rent, utilities, food, and transportation for veterans and their families facing hardship. Available nationwide through local Salvation Army offices.
eligibility: Veterans and families in financial crisis; income verification required at local office

name: Catholic Charities Emergency Assistance
description: Emergency rent, utility, and food assistance regardless of religion. Operates in nearly every US diocese. No military verification required but veterans prioritized in many offices.
eligibility: All individuals facing financial emergency; no religious requirement; priority for veterans in many locations

name: United Way 211 Emergency Referral
description: Dial 211 for free, confidential referral to local emergency services including food banks, rent assistance, utility help, and crisis counseling. Available 24/7 nationwide.
eligibility: All individuals — 211 triages and refers to appropriate local services

name: DAV Emergency Financial Assistance
description: Direct emergency financial grants to disabled veterans for rent, utilities, vehicle repair, and medical expenses. No repayment required.
eligibility: Disabled veterans (VA-rated or awaiting rating) experiencing financial hardship

name: PVA Emergency Financial Assistance
description: One-time emergency grants up to $2,500 for paralyzed and catastrophically disabled veterans facing unexpected financial hardship.
eligibility: Veterans with spinal cord injury/disease or catastrophic disability; PVA membership not required for emergency assistance

name: VFW Unmet Needs Program
description: Grants up to $1,500 for military families facing financial hardship due to deployment, injury, or transition. Covers rent, utilities, vehicle repair, medical, and childcare.
eligibility: Active duty, Guard/Reserve, and recently separated veterans (within 36 months); VFW membership NOT required

name: American Legion Temporary Financial Assistance (TFA)
description: Direct financial grants to minor children of veterans for food, shelter, utilities, and clothing. Designed to prevent family instability.
eligibility: Minor children of active duty, veterans, or deceased veterans; American Legion membership NOT required for TFA

name: Team Red White & Blue Emergency Assistance Fund
description: Emergency grants for veterans facing sudden financial crisis. Covers rent, utilities, and basic needs. Fast turnaround through local chapter network.
eligibility: Veterans experiencing unexpected financial emergency

name: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Federal program providing grants to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered by states. Veterans and military families are priority populations.
eligibility: Households at or below 150% of federal poverty level or 60% of state median income; veterans receive priority in many states

name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Federal food assistance program providing monthly benefits on an EBT card. VA disability pay does NOT count as income for SNAP eligibility.
eligibility: Income-eligible households; VA disability compensation is EXCLUDED from SNAP income calculations

name: Feeding America Veterans Initiative
description: Network of 200+ food banks nationwide with veteran-specific programs. Locator tool finds nearest food bank with no-questions-asked food distribution.
eligibility: All veterans and military families; no income verification required at most locations

name: VA Homeless Veterans Programs (SSVF)
description: Supportive Services for Veteran Families provides rapid re-housing, homelessness prevention, and emergency housing assistance. Includes rent deposits, moving costs, and temporary financial assistance.
eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness; income must be below 50% area median (some at 30%)

name: HUD-VASH (VA Supportive Housing)
description: Combines Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) with VA case management for homeless veterans. Provides long-term rental assistance with wraparound support services.
eligibility: Homeless veterans eligible for VA healthcare; priority for chronically homeless veterans

name: National Call Center for Homeless Veterans
description: 24/7 hotline connecting homeless veterans to VA homeless programs, shelters, and community resources. Staffed by trained counselors.
eligibility: All veterans who are homeless or at risk of homelessness

name: Emergency Assistance for Veterans (VA Social Work)
description: VA medical centers have social workers who can authorize emergency funds for veterans in crisis. Covers prescriptions, transportation, food, and temporary shelter.
eligibility: Veterans enrolled in VA healthcare experiencing acute financial crisis
phone: Contact your local VAMC social work service

name: Modest Needs Self-Sufficiency Grant
description: Grants up to $1,000 for individuals facing a temporary financial crisis that could spiral into larger instability. Covers one specific expense to prevent cascade.
eligibility: Individuals earning above poverty level but facing unexpected expense; must be employed or recently separated from military

name: USDA Emergency Food Assistance (TEFAP)
description: Federal program distributing USDA commodity foods through state agencies and local food banks. Provides shelf-stable food boxes at no cost.
eligibility: Income-eligible households; distributed through local food banks and community organizations

name: St. Vincent de Paul Emergency Aid
description: Local parish-based emergency assistance for rent, utilities, food, and medicine. Available regardless of religion. Operates through home visits to assess need.
eligibility: All individuals in financial distress; no religious requirement; home visit assessment

name: Operation Homefront Critical Financial Assistance
description: Direct financial assistance for active duty, Guard/Reserve, and recently transitioning military families. Covers rent, utilities, vehicle repair, and other critical needs.
eligibility: Active duty E-1 to E-6, Guard/Reserve, and recently separated service members (within 36 months)

name: ESGP (Emergency Solutions Grant Program)
description: Federal HUD grant program funding local organizations providing emergency shelter, rapid re-housing, and homelessness prevention. Veterans receive priority in many communities.
eligibility: Individuals and families who are homeless or at risk of homelessness; administered through local CoC (Continuum of Care) agencies

name: Navy-Marine Corps Relief Society (NMCRS)
description: Interest-free loans and grants for active duty and retired Navy and Marine Corps members. Covers rent, utilities, emergency travel, funeral expenses, and medical bills.
eligibility: Active duty, retired, and Reserve Navy and Marine Corps; surviving spouses and dependents

name: Air Force Aid Society (AFAS)
description: Emergency assistance for Air Force and Space Force members including interest-free loans and grants for basic needs, car repair, and emergency travel.
eligibility: Active duty, retired, Guard/Reserve Air Force and Space Force; surviving spouses and dependents

name: Coast Guard Mutual Assistance (CGMA)
description: Emergency financial assistance for Coast Guard members and families. Interest-free loans and grants for rent, medical, funeral, and emergency travel.
eligibility: Active duty, retired, Reserve Coast Guard; civilian employees of Coast Guard; surviving spouses

name: Purple Heart Foundation Financial Assistance
description: Emergency grants for combat-wounded veterans covering rent, utilities, medical expenses, and emergency transportation.
eligibility: Purple Heart recipients and combat-wounded veterans; proof of combat-related injury required

name: Hope For The Warriors Financial Assistance
description: Immediate financial support for post-9/11 combat-wounded veterans and military families facing crisis. Covers rent, utilities, and transitional needs.
eligibility: Post-9/11 combat-wounded service members, veterans, and their families

name: Veteran Emergency Car Repair Programs
description: Multiple nonprofits provide free or low-cost vehicle repair and donated vehicles for veterans. Vehicle loss is a leading cause of job loss for veterans.
eligibility: Veterans who need vehicle repair or replacement to maintain employment; varies by program

name: Emergency Prescription Assistance (VA)
description: VA pharmacies can provide emergency medication supplies for enrolled veterans. Some VA facilities maintain hardship funds for non-formulary emergency medications.
eligibility: Veterans enrolled in VA healthcare who cannot afford prescriptions or need emergency medication access

name: Emergency Childcare for Veteran Appointments
description: VA medical centers offer free temporary childcare for veterans attending VA medical appointments. Available at many VAMCs through the VA Child Care Program.
eligibility: Veterans with children attending VA medical appointments at participating facilities

name: Veteran Emergency Dental Care
description: Organizations like Dental Lifeline Network and Give Veterans a Smile provide free emergency dental care for veterans who are not eligible for VA dental benefits.
eligibility: Veterans not eligible for VA dental who need emergency dental treatment; income criteria vary by program

name: Utility Shutoff Prevention (State Programs)
description: Most states have utility shutoff moratoriums or emergency assistance programs for households facing disconnection. Many offer veteran-specific expedited processing.
eligibility: Households facing utility disconnection; veterans receive priority in many state programs
phone: Contact state utility commission or dial 211
